description: Transformer architecture internals: attention, MLP, residual stream, normalization, KV cache. The actual operations, not the intuition.
source: Vaswani (2017), Elhage et al. (2021), standard transformer references

triples.subject: transformer
triples.object: alternating attention and MLP layers with residual connections

triples.subject: residual stream
triples.object: the main information highway — each layer reads from and writes back to it

triples.subject: residual stream
triples.object: d_model (hidden size), constant through all layers

triples.subject: self-attention
triples.object: weighted sum of value vectors, where weights come from query-key dot products

triples.subject: self-attention
triples.object: Attention(Q,K,V) = softmax(QK^T / sqrt(d_k)) V

triples.subject: query (Q)
triples.object: W_Q @ residual_stream

triples.subject: key (K)
triples.object: W_K @ residual_stream

triples.subject: value (V)
triples.object: W_V @ residual_stream

triples.subject: attention weights
triples.object: how much each position attends to every other position

triples.subject: attention output
triples.object: weighted combination of value vectors, projected back through W_O

triples.subject: multi-head attention
triples.object: d_model into h heads of dimension d_k = d_model/h

triples.subject: multi-head attention
triples.object: attend to different representation subspaces in parallel

triples.subject: grouped-query attention (GQA)
triples.object: key-value heads across multiple query heads to reduce memory

triples.subject: GQA
triples.object: KV cache is smaller than full multi-head; per-head analysis must respect grouping

triples.subject: KV cache
triples.object: pre-computed key and value tensors from previous tokens to avoid recomputation

triples.subject: KV cache
triples.object: sequence length — n_layers * 2 * n_kv_heads * d_head * seq_len

triples.subject: KV cache
triples.object: deterministic linear projection of the residual stream: K_h = W_K^h x, V_h = W_V^h x (Vaswani et al. 2017)

triples.subject: MLP (feed-forward)
triples.object: nonlinear transformation: W2 @ activation(W1 @ x + b1) + b2

triples.subject: MLP
triples.object: SwiGLU (modern), GELU, or ReLU depending on architecture

triples.subject: MLP
triples.object: stores factual knowledge and computes features (key-value memories interpretation)

triples.subject: layer normalization
triples.object: stabilize training by normalizing activations

triples.subject: RMSNorm
triples.object: simplified layer norm: x / RMS(x) * gain, no centering

triples.subject: RMSNorm
triples.object: input-dependent scaling — NOT purely linear, but approximately linear for small perturbations

triples.subject: pre-norm
triples.object: before attention/MLP (modern default), not after

triples.subject: positional encoding
triples.object: inject position information since attention is permutation-invariant

triples.subject: RoPE (Rotary Position Embedding)
triples.object: rotation to query and key vectors based on position

triples.subject: RoPE
triples.object: pre-RoPE keys are position-independent; post-RoPE keys encode absolute position

triples.subject: RoPE
triples.object: use pre-RoPE keys to avoid position-dependent artifacts in cross-position comparisons

triples.subject: causal masking
triples.object: tokens from attending to future positions

triples.subject: causal masking
triples.object: each position a summary of all previous positions (autoregressive)

triples.subject: last token position
triples.object: the autoregressive summary — attends to all prior tokens, used for next-token prediction

triples.subject: embedding layer
triples.object: token IDs to d_model-dimensional vectors

triples.subject: unembedding (lm_head)
triples.object: final residual stream back to vocabulary logits for next-token prediction

triples.subject: unembedding
triples.object: logits = W_unembed @ final_residual + bias

triples.subject: softmax temperature
triples.object: sharpness of probability distribution — lower = more peaked, higher = more uniform

triples.subject: logits
triples.object: unnormalized scores over vocabulary before softmax

triples.subject: mixture of experts (MoE)
triples.object: each token to a subset of expert MLP layers via a gating network

triples.subject: MoE
triples.object: more parameters with less compute per token (sparse activation)

triples.subject: MoE
triples.object: different inputs activate different experts — routing must be logged for interpretability

triples.subject: residual stream superposition
triples.object: multiple features encoded in overlapping directions (more features than dimensions)

triples.subject: superposition
triples.object: individual features are not axis-aligned, making linear probing incomplete

triples.subject: sparse autoencoders
triples.object: decompose superposed representations into interpretable features

triples.subject: transcoders
triples.object: reconstructing MLP outputs from inputs rather than activations

verification.notes: This is a LOW priority conceptual pack. All 40 triples are factually accurate descriptions of transformer architecture internals. The pack draws correctly from Vaswani et al. (2017), Elhage et al. (2021/2022), and other standard references. The only flagged item is a citation to 'Qasim et al. 2026' which appears to be internal coalition research -- the mathematical claim is correct but the citation may not be externally accessible. The unembedding formula includes a bias term which is technically correct for the general case but many modern LLMs (LLaMA, Mistral) omit the lm_head bias -- this is a minor pedagogical note rather than an error. No numbers, phone numbers, deadlines, or filing fees are present in this pack, consistent with its conceptual/technical nature.

source_urls.description: Vaswani et al. (2017) 'Attention Is All You Need' - the original transformer paper
source_urls.covers_triples: transformer processes_via, self-attention computes/formula, Q/K/V computed_as, attention weights, attention output, multi-head attention splits/purpose, causal masking, embedding layer, unembedding, softmax temperature, logits, positional encoding purpose

source_urls.description: Elhage et al. (2021) 'A Mathematical Framework for Transformer Circuits' - residual stream as information highway, layer read/write semantics
source_urls.covers_triples: residual stream is, residual stream dimension

source_urls.description: Elhage et al. (2022) 'Toy Models of Superposition' - superposition in residual stream, more features than dimensions
Ainslie et al. (2023) 'GQA: Training Generalized Multi-Query Transformer Models from Multi-Head Checkpoints' - grouped-query attention
Su et al. (2021) 'RoFormer: Enhanced Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding' - RoPE mechanics
source_urls.covers_triples: RoPE applies, RoPE implication, RoPE for_analysis

source_urls.description: Zhang and Sennrich (2019) 'Root Mean Square Layer Normalization' - RMSNorm definition and properties
Shazeer (2020) 'GLU Variants Improve Transformer' - SwiGLU and other activation functions for MLP layers
Geva et al. (2021) 'Transformer Feed-Forward Layers Are Key-Value Memories' - MLP as knowledge storage
Shazeer et al. (2017) 'Outrageously Large Neural Networks: The Sparsely-Gated Mixture-of-Experts Layer' - MoE fundamentals
source_urls.covers_triples: mixture of experts (MoE) routes, MoE advantage, MoE implication

source_urls.description: Bricken et al. (2023) 'Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning' - sparse autoencoders for superposition
Dunefsky, Beren, and Neel Nanda (2024) 'Transcoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits' - transcoders vs SAEs for MLP interpretation
Xiong et al. (2020) 'On Layer Normalization in the Transformer Architecture' - pre-norm vs post-norm analysis

pending_corrections.issue: The citation 'Qasim et al. 2026' appears to reference internal/coalition research that may not be externally verifiable by general users. The underlying claim (KV cache is a deterministic projection of the residual stream) is mathematically correct -- K = W_K @ x and V = W_V @ x are deterministic linear transformations. However, the citation may confuse users looking for a standard academic reference. Consider citing this as a mathematical fact derivable from Vaswani (2017) rather than attributing it to a potentially non-public paper.
pending_corrections.corrected_object: deterministic projection of the residual stream (follows directly from the linear projection definitions in Vaswani et al. 2017)
